title: Mobile Push Notification Strategy
tagline: Design a 7-day push notification sequence to reactivate dormant users.
scenario: An e-commerce mobile app in South Africa notices that 40% of users who download the app make one purchase in the first week, but never open the app again. The mobile marketer needs to design a 7-day push campaign that triggers on day 7 of inactivity to bring them back.
brief: Design a 7-day automated push notification flow. Write the actual copy, select trigger timings, set target screens (deep-linking rules), and define control groups for testing.
deliverables: The flow schedule (days and times) and segment specifications (e.g., users inactive for 7-14 days with historical purchase)
Copy for 4 push notifications (Title, Body, Emoji) optimized for lock screen readability
The deep-link mapping for each push notification (where in the app the user lands when they tap)
The experiment design: how you would measure lift against a holdout control group
submissionGuidance: Push notifications are highly intrusive. Too many will lead to app uninstalls. Keep the copy short (under 90 characters), hyper-personalized, and value-focused.

rubric.label: Push Notification Copy
rubric.description: Is the copy engaging, concise, and direct about the incentive?

rubric.label: Timing & Deep Links
rubric.description: Are the trigger timings logical and do deep links drop the user in the correct high-value screens?

rubric.label: Experimentation Design
rubric.description: Is the control group methodology sound to prove incremental lift?
